title: PeriodOfTime
description: Information about a specific time period with a start- and/or end-time

endDate.title: end date
endDate.description: The end date of the period of time

anyOf.description: A year and month in YYYY-MM format

_oldDocs.range: rdfs:Literal (typed as xsd:date, xsd:dateTime, xsd:gYear or xsd:gYearMonth)

startDate.title: start date
startDate.description: The start date of the period of time

anyOf.description: A year and month in YYYY-MM format

_oldDocs.range: rdfs:Literal (typed as xsd:date, xsd:dateTime, xsd:gYear or xsd:gYearMonth)

_oldDocs.rationale: The introduction of dcterms:PeriodOfTime in DCAT-US 3.0 is pivotal for harmonizing with international standards and rectifying the inconsistency with DCAT 1. In DCAT-US 1.1, [[ISO8601-1]] was used for interval representation in dcterms:temporal, diverging from DCAT 1's requirement of dcterms:PeriodOfTime. This alignment with DCAT 3 standards in DCAT-US 3.0 not only resolves discrepancies but also streamlines data processing, simplifying parsing and indexing of time intervals. By adopting dcterms:PeriodOfTime, DCAT-US 3.0 promotes ease of implementation, ensuring uniformity, flexibility, accuracy, and enhanced interoperability in handling time-related data, ultimately benefiting data usability and exchange.
